metaTitle: Special Interfaith Court Marriage Bangladesh 2026 Process Guide
title: Special Marriage & Interfaith Marriage in Bangladesh 2026: Legal Process & Registrar Guide
metaDescription: Complete guide to interfaith marriage & court marriage in Bangladesh under Special Marriage Act 1872 — 14-day notice, registrar fees, affidavit, legal validity abroad, and protection of rights.
heroIntro: In Bangladesh, while traditional marriages are solemnized under personal religious laws (Muslim, Hindu, Christian, Buddhist), couples of different faiths who do not wish to convert to another religion can legally marry under the Special Marriage Act 1872. Known commonly as 'Special Court Marriage', this statutory framework allows interfaith couples, atheists, or civil partners to register a legally binding marriage before an appointed Marriage Registrar. For confidential legal guidance and affidavit drafting, consult Supreme Court Advocate Advocate Md. Shah Alam.

quickAnswer.heading: ⚡ Key Facts: Special Marriage Act 1872 in Bangladesh
points: No Religious Conversion Required: Couples of different religions can marry while retaining their respective personal beliefs.
14-Day Mandatory Notice: Written notice must be submitted to the Marriage Registrar 14 days prior.
Age Limit: Male must be at least 21 years old; Female must be at least 18 years old.
Witness Requirement: Must have 3 adult witnesses sign the Special Marriage Register.
International Validity: Certificate issued under Special Marriage Act is 100% recognized by foreign embassies for spouse visas.

sections.h2: What Is the Special Marriage Act 1872 in Bangladesh?
sections.content:
The Special Marriage Act 1872 (Act III of 1872) is a civil marriage statute in Bangladesh designed for individuals who profess different religions (e.g., Hindu-Muslim, Christian-Hindu, Buddhist-Muslim) or who declare they do not profess any specific religion.

Unlike Muslim Nikahnama or Hindu Shastric rites, Special Marriage is a purely civil contract administered by a government-appointed Special Marriage Registrar.

Consult an experienced Family Lawyer in Dhaka to ensure all statutory steps are executed smoothly.

To qualify for marriage under the Special Marriage Act 1872, the parties must fulfill these legal requirements:

  • Age Requirement: The groom must be at least 21 years of age; the bride must be at least 18 years of age.
  • Marital Status: Neither party must have a living spouse at the time of marriage (monogamy requirement).
  • Mental Capacity: Both parties must be capable of giving valid free consent.
  • Prohibited Degrees of Consanguinity: The parties must not be related within prohibited degrees of relationship.

sections.h2: Step 1: The Mandatory 14-Day Public Notice to Marriage Registrar
sections.content:
Under Section 4 & 6 of the Special Marriage Act 1872, one of the parties must reside in the district of the Special Marriage Registrar for at least 14 days before submitting notice.

Process:

  1. Submit written Notice of Intended Marriage (ফরম-ক) to the Special Marriage Registrar.
  2. The Registrar files the notice in the Marriage Notice Book and displays a copy at their office for 14 consecutive days.
  3. If no valid legal objection is received within 14 days, the marriage can be solemnized anytime within 3 months.

sections.h2: Step 2: Declaration Affidavit before First Class Magistrate / Notary
sections.content:
Along with registrar notice, the couple must execute a sworn joint Declaration Affidavit (হলফনামা) before a First Class Magistrate or Notary Public in Dhaka:

  • Stating free voluntary consent without any coercion, threat, or kidnapping.
  • Affirming age proof (NID / Passport / SSC Certificate).
  • Affirming single status (unmarried/divorced/widowed).
  • Declaring intent to marry under Special Marriage Act III of 1872.

sections.h2: Step 3: Solemnization Ceremony & Special Marriage Register Entry
sections.content:
After the 14-day notice period expires:

  • The couple appears before the Special Marriage Registrar along with 3 adult witnesses.
  • Both parties sign the statutory declaration in the presence of the Registrar and witnesses.
  • The Registrar enters details into the official Special Marriage Register and issues the government Marriage Certificate.

sections.h2: Is Religious Conversion Mandatory for Interfaith Marriage?
sections.content:
No! This is the most crucial aspect of the Special Marriage Act 1872. Neither partner is legally required to change their name or convert to another religion. Both partners retain their original religious identity or non-religious status.

However, under Section 2 to 4, parties sign a declaration stating they do not profess specific religious personal laws for the purpose of this civil marriage contract.

sections.h2: Inheritance Rights of Children Born to Special Marriage Partners
sections.content:
Under Section 22 to 25 of the Special Marriage Act 1872:

  • Severance from Joint Family: Marriage under this Act operates as a severance from any joint Hindu or religious family system.
  • Inheritance Law: Succession to property of persons married under this Act, and the property of their issue (children), is governed exclusively by the Indian Succession Act 1925 (civil inheritance) rather than personal religious laws.
  • Children born to special marriage partners are 100% legitimate under Bangladesh law and hold full legal inheritance rights.

sections.content:

faqs.question: Is a court marriage affidavit on 200 Taka stamp alone a valid marriage in Bangladesh?
faqs.answer: No! A notary affidavit alone is NOT a legal marriage. It is merely a declaration of intent. A valid marriage requires registration before an official Nikah Registrar (for Muslims) or Special Marriage Registrar (for interfaith under Special Marriage Act 1872).

faqs.question: Is a Special Marriage certificate recognized for foreign spouse visas (US, UK, Canada)?
faqs.answer: Yes. Foreign embassies and immigration authorities fully recognize Marriage Certificates issued by the Special Marriage Registrar under the Special Marriage Act 1872.

faqs.question: Can parents stop an adult couple from doing court marriage in Bangladesh?
faqs.answer: No. Any male aged 21+ and female aged 18+ is a legal adult. If both freely consent, parents cannot legally stop or invalidate the marriage. Attempts to file false abduction cases can be quashed in court.

faqs.question: Can a Muslim man and a Hindu woman marry without conversion?
faqs.answer: Yes. They can legally marry under the Special Marriage Act 1872 without either party converting.
